explanation: The natural language statement 'system diagnosis requires more explanation' suggests that whenever the system is performing a diagnosis, there must also be a detailed explanation provided (either simultaneously or as a persistent condition while diagnosis is occurring). In LTL, this can be represented as 'G(systemDiagnosis -> detailed)', which means 'always, if the system is performing a diagnosis, then a detailed explanation is provided at that time.' This formalizes the requirement that detailed explanations must coincide with system diagnoses. No previous LTL property was given, so no comparison is needed.

The natural language states that when the system diagnosis occurs, it requires more explanation. This suggests that whenever 'systemDiagnosis' is true, 'detailed' must also be true at that time. The corresponding LTL property for this is 'G(systemDiagnosis -> detailed)', which globally requires that at every point where the system is performing a diagnosis, a detailed explanation is present as well. This directly captures the requirement without misrepresenting the timing or presence of other propositions; it simply states that 'detailed' must accompany 'systemDiagnosis' always.

The requirement 'system diagnosis requires more explanation' can be interpreted as: whenever the system is performing a diagnosis, it should be accompanied by a detailed explanation. In LTL, this is captured by 'G(systemDiagnosis -> detailed)', which means globally, if a system diagnosis is being performed, then a detailed explanation must also hold at that time. This directly and faithfully maps the natural language input to LTL without introducing additional requirements or assumptions.

The natural language states that when the system diagnosis occurs, there must be more explanation, which is interpreted as the explanation being detailed. This can be formalized in LTL as: whenever the system is performing a diagnosis, it must also be the case that the explanation provided is detailed. The formula 'G(systemDiagnosis -> detailed)' expresses that globally, every time 'systemDiagnosis' holds, 'detailed' must hold as well. This ensures that a detailed explanation always accompanies any system diagnosis, as required by the input.

The natural language statement 'system diagnosis requires more explanation' suggests that whenever the system is performing a diagnosis, there must be a detailed explanation accompanying it. This can be represented in LTL as: globally, whenever 'systemDiagnosis' holds, 'detailed' must also hold. The formula G(systemDiagnosis -> detailed) mandates that at all times if a diagnosis is taking place, then a detailed explanation is available at that very moment. This matches the intent that 'requires more explanation' refers to an obligation that is always present during diagnosis, not merely eventually or at some other time.

The natural language statement 'system diagnosis requires more explanation' can be interpreted as: whenever the system is performing a diagnosis, a detailed explanation must be provided at that same time. That means globally, at all points where systemDiagnosis holds, detailed must also hold. In LTL, this is best represented as 'G(systemDiagnosis -> detailed)'. This guarantees that there is no point in the execution where a diagnosis is being performed without a detailed explanation at that moment. Alternative LTL properties (such as 'systemDiagnosis U detailed' or 'F(systemDiagnosis & detailed)') would either allow diagnosis without immediate detail or not enforce the requirement synchronously, which does not capture the need for a detailed explanation whenever a diagnosis occurs.

The natural language statement 'system diagnosis requires more explanation' suggests that whenever the system is performing a diagnosis, there should also be a detailed explanation provided at the same time. In LTL, this relationship is best captured as 'globally, if systemDiagnosis holds, then detailed must also hold' (G(systemDiagnosis -> detailed)). This ensures that at every point in time when system diagnosis occurs, a detailed explanation accompanies it. Alternate LTL forms that do not require the detailed explanation to co-occur (such as eventuality of detailed after diagnosis, or implication in the other direction) would not fully capture the requirement for more (i.e., always sufficient) explanation during every diagnosis.

The phrase 'system diagnosis requires more explanation' suggests that whenever the system is diagnosing, a detailed explanation must be provided at the same time. In LTL, this means that globally (G), whenever 'systemDiagnosis' holds, 'detailed' must also hold at that point. This is expressed as G(systemDiagnosis -> detailed). This property enforces that during any diagnosis, there is always a detailed explanation.

The natural language statement 'system diagnosis requires more explanation' can be interpreted to mean that whenever the system is performing a diagnosis, a detailed explanation must also be provided at the same time. In this context, the translation can be captured by the formula G(systemDiagnosis -> detailed), which expresses that globally, at every point in time where a diagnosis is occurring, a detailed explanation is required. This ensures that there cannot be a diagnosis without a detailed explanation; thus, it directly captures the demand that a diagnosis 'requires more explanation.'

The natural language statement suggests that whenever a system diagnosis occurs, a more detailed explanation is required. This is best captured in LTL by stating that globally, whenever 'systemDiagnosis' is true, eventually (within any future point) 'detailed' will also become true. In LTL, this is written as G(systemDiagnosis -> F detailed), expressing that every time a system diagnosis happens, it is eventually followed by a detailed explanation. This covers the requirement in the provided input.
